{
  "analysis": "We have OCR for all frames and dense 5fps bottom OCR complete (951 files). The ending OCR explicitly states 262 moves, so the final solution must contain exactly 262 command lines. Current candidate logs are undercounted/noisy (e.g., move_increment has 237 entries, dense_v2 likely noisy). The judge guidance is correct: first inspect the tail (frames 180-190) to confirm final move count and trailing commands, then run a robust dense extractor over the 5fps bottom OCR with OCR fixes and segment-based submit detection, inspect the candidate count/content, and iterate toward exactly 262 before writing /app/solution.txt. No final file should be written yet.",
  "plan": "Inspect OCR for frames 180-190 to confirm the ending move count and tail commands. Then create and run a dense 5fps submit detector that treats each contiguous typed prompt segment as a submitted command, applies OCR fixes, filters obvious game-text fragments, deduplicates consecutive identical commands only, maps to 1fps frame indices, and writes `/app/work/logs/dense_v3.log` with a count. This is intermediate validation; no solution.txt will be written until the count is exactly 262 and the sequence is coherent.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "python3 - <<'PY'
import glob, os, re
fs=sorted(glob.glob('/app/work/ocr/frame_*.txt'), key=lambda p:int(os.path.basename(p).split('_')[1].split('.')[0]))
for idx,p in enumerate(fs[-11:], len(fs)-10):
    txt=open(p,errors='ignore').read()
    m=re.search(r'Moves?:\s*(\d+)', txt)
    mv=m.group(1) if m else 'None'
    print(f'\n==== frame {idx} moves={mv} ====')
    print(txt[:2500])
PY
",
      "duration": 1.0
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "cat > /app/work/dense_extractor_v3.py <<'PY'
import glob, os, re

fs=sorted(glob.glob('/app/work/bottom5/frame_*.txt'), key=lambda p:int(os.path.basename(p).split('_')[1].split('.')[0]))

fixes={
    'gY':'y','Y':'y','G':'again','g':'again',
    'move,':'move rug','open tra':'open trap',
    'ulysse':'ulysses','ulyss':'ulysses',
    'pu,':'put','pu':'put',
    'Dopen':'open','dopen':'open',
    'elose':'close','ceage':'cage','eage':'cage','ca_':'cage','ca':'cage',
    'mirro':'mirror',
    'temp':'temple','tem':'temple','tem_':'temple',
    'rin':'ring','dro':'drop',
    'get all fr':'get all from cage','get all from _':'get all from cage','get all from ca_':'get all from cage',
    'get lamp,clove':'get lamp,clove,screwdriver','get lamp,clove,sc':'get lamp,clove,screwdriver','get lamp,clove,screw':'get lamp,clove,screwdriver',
    'put torch,scr':'put torch,screwdriver','put torch,screw':'put torch,screwdriver',
    'get huge, tor':'get huge,torch','get huge,torc':'get huge,torch','get nuge,torcn':'get huge,torch','get huge,tor':'get huge,torch',
    'get egg,sow':'get egg,sword,lamp','get egg, sword,1':'get egg,sword,lamp',
    'get head, jade,cup,egg,go':'get head,jade,cup,egg,golden',
    'get head, jade,cup,egg, golden':'get head,jade,cup,egg,golden',
    'get head, jade,cup,egg,gold':'get head,jade,cup,egg,golden',
    'get head, Jade ,cup,egg,goiden':'get head,jade,cup,egg,golden',
    'Wind ac':'wind canary','Wind canary':'wind canary','win':'wind canary','win_':'wind canary',
    '1li':'li','1lid':'lid','Lid':'lid','lid':'lid',
    'wW':'w','e,':'e','d.':'d','ne.':'ne',
    'n.':'n',
    'case':'case',
    'put all':'put all in case',
}

def normalize(s):
    s=re.sub(r'[^A-Za-z0-9>, ._]',' ',s)
    s=re.sub(r'\s+',' ',s).strip()
    if s.startswith('>'):
        s=s[1:].strip()
    s=s.rstrip('._').strip()
    if s in fixes:
        s=fixes[s]
    return s

lines=[]
for idx,p in enumerate(fs,1):
    raw=open(p,errors='ignore').read().strip()
    lines.append(normalize(raw))

# Segment-based submit detector: contiguous non-empty prompt lines are one typed command.
segments=[]
cur=None
for i,line in enumerate(lines,1):
    if line:
        if cur is None:
            cur=(i,line)
        elif line.startswith(cur[1]) or (len(cur[1])>=3 and line.startswith(cur[1][:-1])):
            cur=(i,line)
        elif cur[1].startswith(line) and len(cur[1])-len(line)<=3:
            pass
        else:
            segments.append(cur)
            cur=(i,line)
    else:
        if cur:
            segments.append(cur)
            cur=None
if cur:
    segments.append(cur)

# Filter obvious game-text fragments: no letters or long narrative strings.
filtered=[]
for i,c in segments:
    if not re.search(r'[A-Za-z]', c):
        continue
    if len(c) > 50 and ' ' in c:
        continue
    filtered.append((i,c))

# Deduplicate consecutive identical submits only.
final=[]
for i,c in filtered:
    if final and final[-1][1] == c:
        continue
    final.append((i,c))

def b5_to_1fps(i):
    return max(1, min(190, (i+4)//5))

with open('/app/work/logs/dense_v3.log','w') as f:
    for n,(i,c) in enumerate(final,1):
        f.write(f'{n:03d} b5={i:05d} f={b5_to_1fps(i):03d} {c}\n')

print('dense_v3_count', len(final))
print('expected', 262)
print('first30', final[:30])
print('last30', final[-30:])
PY
python3 /app/work/dense_extractor_v3.py
wc -l /app/work/logs/dense_v3.log
sed -n '1,140p' /app/work/logs/dense_v3.log
",
      "duration": 1.0
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "sed -n '141,300p' /app/work/logs/dense_v3.log
",
      "duration": 0.1
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "sed -n '301,520p' /app/work/logs/dense_v3.log; tail -40 /app/work/logs/dense_v3.log
",
      "duration": 0.1
    }
  ]
}
